research_topic: Research B - missed signals, diagnostic errors, and late discovery cases
setting_or_context: primary care; specialist clinic; hospital outpatient
review_or_complaint_excerpt_short: Source reports she felt made to feel 'she was crazy.'
complaint_context: Severe cyclic pain normalized and split between GI and gynecology.
process_step: medical history; first clinician assessment; referral; specialist review
product_or_system_implication: Create menstrual + GI timeline capture, severity thresholds, and cross-specialty prompts for persistent dysmenorrhea.
notes: Case shows patient and mother advocacy as a diagnostic driver.

research_topic: Research B - missed signals, diagnostic errors, and late discovery cases
setting_or_context: emergency department; primary care; specialist clinic
review_or_complaint_excerpt_short: Patient explicitly asked if it could be a heart rhythm problem.
complaint_context: Intermittent conditions can disappear during testing and become labeled psychiatric.
process_step: first clinician assessment; labs/imaging interpretation; referral; repeat visit
product_or_system_implication: Offer event-triggered monitoring workflows and a 'normal when asymptomatic' warning for episodic cardiopulmonary complaints.
notes: Source notes PSVT symptoms are often attributed to panic/anxiety, especially in women.

research_topic: Research B - missed signals, diagnostic errors, and late discovery cases
setting_or_context: primary care; specialist clinic; emergency department
review_or_complaint_excerpt_short: Source says patient felt the urology clinician was dismissive and berating.
complaint_context: A high-risk measurement lacked immediate safety action and patient education.
process_step: medical history; specialist review; labs ordered; follow-up; emergency escalation
product_or_system_implication: Create hard-stop follow-up for extreme post-void residual: creatinine, catheter plan, teach-back, and owner assignment.
notes: Case shows relational rupture causing loss to follow-up.

research_topic: Research B - missed signals, diagnostic errors, and late discovery cases
source_platform_or_type: News report with legal admission/public complaint
setting_or_context: public health screening; specialist clinic; hospital outpatient
review_or_complaint_excerpt_short: Patient urged others to 'trust their instincts.'
complaint_context: Screening result and symptom follow-up failed over years.
process_step: public health screening; lab interpretation; diagnosis discussion; follow-up
product_or_system_implication: Screening safety layer: patient-visible abnormal-history timeline, second-review triggers, and symptom/result reconciliation.

research_topic: Research B - missed signals, diagnostic errors, and late discovery cases
review_or_complaint_excerpt_short: Patients described dismissal of their experience.
complaint_context: New chronic condition lacked stable diagnostic workflow and validation.
process_step: first clinician assessment; medical history; diagnosis discussion; referral; long-term monitoring
product_or_system_implication: Capture patient-generated longitudinal symptom burden and prevent closure solely because routine tests are normal.
notes: Not one case; included as documented patient-voice pattern.

research_topic: Research B - missed signals, diagnostic errors, and late discovery cases
setting_or_context: primary care; emergency department; specialist clinic
review_or_complaint_excerpt_short: Survey reports many were told symptoms were 'all in your head.'
complaint_context: A simple orthostatic pattern is missed when symptoms are separated by specialty.
process_step: medical history; first clinician assessment; referral; specialist review; long-term monitoring
product_or_system_implication: Add orthostatic-vital workflow and patient-triggered POTS screener for recurrent tachycardia/dizziness/fatigue complaints.
notes: Survey has advocacy-source limitations but valuable patient voice and numeric delay pattern.

research_topic: Research B - missed signals, diagnostic errors, and late discovery cases
setting_or_context: primary care; hospital inpatient; home/community visit
review_or_complaint_excerpt_short: She asked for help to 'get enough food to live.'
complaint_context: Functional decline and care needs fell between medical and social-care boundaries.
process_step: before visit; first clinician assessment; inpatient assessment; discharge; long-term monitoring
product_or_system_implication: Track functional intake, hydration, and care capacity as clinical red flags, not just social notes.
notes: Highly sensitive case; included with minimal public details.

research_topic: Research B - missed signals, diagnostic errors, and late discovery cases
review_or_complaint_excerpt_short: Patient wrote that anxiety was used against her.
complaint_context: Basic normal labs blocked deeper autoimmune evaluation despite systemic pattern.
process_step: medical history; labs ordered; lab interpretation; referral; patient self-advocacy
product_or_system_implication: Give patients structured way to upload symptom photos, family history, and flare timelines; flag repeated systemic symptoms even with normal basic labs.
notes: Patient-story evidence; useful for voice/pattern, not incidence.

research_topic: Research B - missed signals, diagnostic errors, and late discovery cases
setting_or_context: primary care; specialist clinic; inpatient ward; emergency department
review_or_complaint_excerpt_short: Story attributes delay partly to being a young woman.
complaint_context: Age/gender assumptions diverted GI disease into behavioral explanation.
process_step: medical history; referral; specialist review; repeat visit; emergency escalation
product_or_system_implication: Use red-flag pediatric/adolescent GI triggers: weight loss, fractures/malnutrition, recurrent admissions, infections, and persistent abdominal pain.
notes: Patient-story evidence; included for bias/young-woman workflow pattern.
